title: Charred and Juicy Canadian Poutine Gratin
description: A nostalgic twist on the classic Canadian dish featuring charred, juicy, and creamy elements. Perfect for a cozy dinner or night in with friends.
instructions: Prepare the potatoes by peeling and cutting them into 1/2-inch thick rounds.
Soak the potato slices in cold water for at least 30 minutes to remove excess starch.
Drain and pat dry the potato slices, then toss with beef tallow and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Char the potatoes in two batches in a large skillet over medium-high heat for about 3-4 minutes per side. Set aside.
In a small saucepan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over low heat. Whisk in Beurre-Monte and cook for 1-2 minutes to thicken.
Add the Tarragon-Infused French Onion Soup Mix and whisk until combined.
In a greased 9x13-inch baking dish, arrange the charred potatoes in a single layer.
Pour the Beurre-Monte mixture evenly over the potatoes, then sprinkle with chopped tarragon and grated cheese (if using).
Dot the top with remaining butter and cover the dish with aluminum foil.
Bake for 25 minutes, then remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the potatoes are tender.
Let rest for 5 minutes before serving and garnishing with extra tarragon leaves, if desired.
